_comment: Three-level UCAN delegation chain demonstrating capability attenuation. Human principal delegates broad authority to a Personal Agent, which sub-delegates a narrow subset to a Specialist Agent. Each level can only narrow capabilities, never expand them.

delegations._comment: Level 0: Human principal delegates to Personal Agent. Broadest scope.

fct.delegationPurpose: Full agent management of zone applications and payments

delegations._comment: Level 1: Personal Agent sub-delegates to Specialist Agent. Narrower scope: only zone-a residency, lower payment cap, no wildcard document access.

att._comment: Attenuated: same zone, same action, but lower maxValue (5000 vs parent's 10000)
Attenuated: documents only in zone-a (parent had wildcard *)
Attenuated: lower payment caps (2500/tx and 10000/mo vs parent's 5000/tx and 25000/mo)

fct.delegationPurpose: Specialist agent for zone-a residency applications

delegations._comment: Verification chain: Human (LEVEL_3) -> Personal Agent (full scope) -> Specialist Agent (zone-a only, reduced caps). The prf array in level 1 references the hash of the level 0 token, proving the delegation chain is unbroken. A verifier checks: (1) level 1 capabilities are a subset of level 0, (2) level 0 was issued by the principal, (3) all signatures verify.
